mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income that supports aggregate demand and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and compresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.5% is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating consumer prices and a rising federal funds rate signal stagflationary monetary tightening that erodes profit margins and valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are rising, positive 20-day SPX returns occur in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals that restrictive policy is successfully suppressing real output, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified tightening cycles, month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's rank IC is negative during tightening but positive during easing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in total retail sales signals an abrupt contraction in consumer demand that immediately lowers near-term revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS decreases coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances during any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the mean return during such episodes is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously falling industrial production and rising unemployment confirm a broad supply and demand contraction that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and increases in UNRATE fail to predict negative 60-day returns (positive returns in > 50% of joint-signal observations), or the interaction term does not improve prediction over additive main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP confirms an economy-wide contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over any 5-year window.
